title: Ebola scare: Man from Congo, 3 others isolated in Ahmedabad; samples sent for testing
content: A man from Congo and three contacts have been isolated in Ahmedabad for Ebola testing. This follows intensified screening of passengers from affected African nations at the airport. While India has no reported cases, Gujarat has prepared isolation beds as a precaution.

title: Beyond kamikaze strikes: These 17 Ukrainian UAVs are changing drone warfare
content: Ukraine is revolutionizing drone warfare, producing millions of advanced aerial, naval, and ground systems. Innovations include AI-guided interceptors, jet-powered strike drones for deep penetration, and naval kamikaze vessels. The widespread adoption of parachute recovery systems signifies a strategic shift towards reusable, cost-effective drone operations on the modern battlefield.

title: Cloudflare lays off 1,100 employees; CEO says company isn’t struggling
content: Cloudflare is laying off over 1,100 employees, about 20% of its workforce, to restructure around AI adoption. CEO Matthew Prince explained the cuts target 'measurers' – roles like middle management and operations – as AI enhances efficiency. He believes AI will boost productivity for 'builders' and 'sellers', allowing greater investment in growth areas.

title: 'Something going profoundly wrong':Suicides among ICE detainees hit record high
content: An Associated Press investigation reveals a surge in detainee suicides within ICE custody since January 2025, with at least 10 men dying by self-harm. Experts cite systemic failures in mental health care, delayed screenings, and isolation contributing to these preventable deaths.

title: Sooryavanshi breaks Chris Gayle’s IPL record, hits most sixes in a season
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has etched his name in IPL history, smashing Chris Gayle's 14-year-old record for most sixes in a season during the Eliminator. He also became the youngest to score 600 runs and the first to achieve this volume with a strike rate over 200.  This season is truly one for the ages.

title: Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Indian banks at higher risk from Middle East crisis, says Moody's
content: Moody's Ratings has identified Indian banks as highly exposed to Middle East crisis risks due to the nation's significant energy import dependence. Sustained high oil prices could pressure inflation, interest rates, and borrower cash flows, impacting loan quality. Despite these challenges, Indian banks possess adequate capital and provisioning buffers to absorb potential credit losses.

title: Woman’s body found stuffed inside blue drum in Punjab; tattoo helps identify victim
content: Police in Patiala recovered the body of 25-year-old Neha, found stuffed inside a blue plastic drum near the DMW Railway Colony. Her family identified her through social media photographs. Two local youths, known drug users often seen with the victim, have been detained for questioning.

title: Was Annamalai's rare dissent a message to BJP leadership in Delhi?
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP chief K Annamalai publicly opposed the CBSE's revised three-language mandate, urging its withdrawal due to abrupt implementation. This move signals a calibrated political strategy to assert regional concerns and navigate the sensitive language issue in Tamil Nadu, potentially influencing the party's future leadership.

title: European companies may not be 'listening' to their governments' 'worries' on China: Survey
content: European firms are deepening their presence in China. A survey shows most companies are keeping or growing operations there. Many are even increasing their supply chain activities within China. This trend highlights a disconnect between political calls to reduce reliance on Beijing and the economic realities faced by businesses. Companies see China as crucial for global competitiveness.

title: Balen Shah's two-month report card: Why Nepal’s Gen Z hero is under fire already
content: Two months into office, Nepal's Prime Minister Balendra Shah faces scrutiny as his ambitious reform agenda lags, with key promises marked "overdue." His administration grapples with cabinet instability, legal challenges over decisions, and criticism of his governing style, including bypassing institutions and controversial enforcement actions.

title: She met her ex once, her husband calls it adultery: Here is what the court said
content: A Punjab and Haryana High Court ruling clarifies that a single meeting with a former partner doesn't prove adultery. The court upheld a divorce granted on cruelty grounds, finding a wife's unsubstantiated allegations against her husband and his family constituted mental cruelty, despite rejecting the adultery claim.

title: What makes INS Kolkata formidable? The warship that foiled a piracy threat in the Indian Ocean
content: Inside INS Kolkata, the Indian Navy warship that foiled a piracy threat in the Indian OceanThe Indian Navy on Wednesday successfully foiled a suspected piracy attempt near merchant vessel MV Mashallah 1 in the Western Indian Ocean, once again putting spotlight on India’s growing maritime surveillance and rapid response capabilities in one of the world’s most strategically sensitive sea routes. The operation was carried out by Indian Naval Ship (INS) Kolkata, which is currently deployed in the Western Indian Ocean near the Gulf of Aden as part of the Navy’s anti-piracy mission.

title: 4 CPM workers arrested after attack on ED car during protest over raids at former Kerala CM’s house
content: CPI-M workers were arrested for allegedly attacking ED officials during protests outside former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan's residence. The ED conducted searches in connection with the CMRL case, which Vijayan and Arvind Kejriwal criticized as a "targeted attack" and misuse of central agencies.

title: The trillion-dollar countdown: SpaceX’s race for the largest IPO in human history
content: SpaceX is preparing for a historic IPO, aiming for a $1.75-$2 trillion valuation and seeking to raise $75 billion by June 2026. Despite significant revenue growth from Starlink, the company reported substantial losses in 2025 and Q1 2026, largely due to heavy investments in its new AI unit following the xAI acquisition.

title: ‘I am unfit to play’- RR captain Riyan Parag drops shocking update at toss
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad elected to bowl first against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL 2026 Eliminator. RR skipper Riyan Parag revealed he's not fully fit but stressed the team's win-or-go-home mentality. SRH captain Pat Cummins expressed confidence in his team's recent form, aiming for simple bowling. Both teams have had mirroring seasons, with SRH holding a 2-0 advantage over RR this year.

title: Indian Idol 16: Vishal Dadlani replaced from singing reality show? Singer breaks silence
content: Amidst swirling whispers about Vishal Dadlani's departure from Indian Idol 16 linked to his bold comments on the NEET-UG paper leak, the artist set the record straight with a cheeky video straight from the stage, playfully advising followers to ignore the internet's buzz. He highlighted the necessity of voicing one's opinions, winning admiration from fans for his bravery.

title: Mekedatu dam & Tamil Nadu anthem: What Vijay discussed with PM Modi in 1st meeting as CM
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to block Karnataka's Mekedatu dam project without the consent of downstream states, citing farmer anxieties. He also sought clarity on singing the state anthem at government functions and requested the establishment of a Centre for Airborne Systems.

title: Zubeen Garg death case: Court frames murder charges against four accused,
content: A fast-track court in Assam has framed murder charges against four of the seven accused in singer Zubeen Garg's death case, with the trial set to begin June 8. This comes despite a Singapore court ruling his September 2025 death an accidental drowning with no foul play involved.

title: Karnataka CM tussle: Congress MLA says Siddaramaiah to quit tomorrow; DKS camp celebrates
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah is reportedly set to resign on Thursday after 3 PM, with Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar likely to take over. Congress MLA Ashok K Pattan indicated the decision follows directives from the party's high command. Supporters of Shivakumar celebrated across the state amid intense speculation about a leadership change.

title: Happy Eid-ul-Adha 2026: Top 100 Eid Mubarak wishes and quotes for Bakrid
content: Navigating the flurry of Eid-ul-Adha greetings can be overwhelming. This guide offers 100 ready-to-use messages, from heartfelt family wishes and foodie friend prompts to professional greetings and quick replies.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Bhopal court remands Samarth Singh to CBI custody, says lawyer
content: Twisha Sharma's husband, Samarth Singh, has been remanded to CBI custody in Bhopal following the agency's takeover of the former model-actor's death investigation. Sharma was found hanging at her marital home on May 12. Her family alleges harassment and domestic violence by her in-laws, who are named as accused.

title: ‘Virat Kohli is not human’- Ashwin’s hilarious yet stunning praise goes viral
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a dominant victory over Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1, advancing to the final. Ravichandran Ashwin lauded Virat Kohli's electrifying on-field intensity and praised Krunal Pandya's crucial contribution. RCB's bowlers then stifled GT's chase, ensuring a comfortable win and a shot at defending their title.

title: Why India’s Chungreng Koren’s UFC dream resonates beyond his own journey
content: India’s Chungreng Koren has battled hardship for most of his life, from personal tragedy and poverty to career setbacks and inner demons. Determined to build a better future through MMA, the Manipuri fighter relentlessly pursued a dream that nearly slipped away through circumstances beyond his control. Now, with another chance at Road to UFC, Koren stands on the cusp of history once again. But for him, this journey is no longer just about personal ambition; it is about carrying the hopes of his family, his people and Indian MMA forward.

title: Trump’s Board of Peace has ‘zero’ funds despite billion-dollar pledges: Report
content: Donald Trump's Board of Peace, established to aid Gaza's reconstruction, has received zero dollars in its official fund despite billions pledged by member nations. Donations are reportedly being directed to a JPMorgan account lacking independent transparency requirements, impacting plans for a US-backed Palestinian administration.

title: Patidar ahead of Iyer? R Ashwin’s big endorsement for RCB skipper
content: Rajat Patidar's explosive 93 not out against Gujarat Titans propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a record IPL playoff total. His fearless batting, including a remarkable backfoot six off Kagiso Rabada, earned high praise from Ravichandran Ashwin, who hailed it as a potential shot of the tournament. Patidar's dominant display has elevated his captaincy and batting credentials significantly.

title: Why goats in Mumbai's Mira Road housing society sparked standoff ahead of Bakrid
content: A dispute over housing goats for Bakrid rituals inside a Mira Road housing society escalated into protests and a bizarre pig-carrying incident. Objections arose over animal sacrifice within residential premises, leading to a standoff. Police intervened, and goats were eventually relocated to a designated municipal site, restoring order.

title: Eid wishes: 20 images that you can share with your friends and family
content: Eid celebrations now blend tradition with digital connection. While prayers and family gatherings remain central, sending wishes online via WhatsApp and social media has become a modern ritual. These digital greetings, often accompanied by images, help maintain connections, especially for those far from home, ensuring no one feels forgotten during this time of gratitude and togetherness.

title: Quote of the day: The formula Federer used every time life left him 'stuck in hole'
content: Roger Federer’s reflection on adversity captures the mindset that defined his career and comeback. After injury setbacks and doubts over his future, Federer returned stronger, proving resilience can reshape failure into growth. His message extends far beyond tennis, arguing that difficult periods, whether in sport, work or life, often force the reinvention, patience and discipline that ultimately make people stronger than before.

title: Mira Road Bakrid row: Is animal sacrifice inside a housing society legal?
content: In a surprising turn of events on Mira Road, a conflict sparked over the presence of goats brought into a housing society for Bakrid festivities, resulting in public demonstrations and police involvement. While the right to religious sacrifice is safeguarded by law, municipal regulations strictly prohibit the act within residential properties.

title: TMC MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar steps down from all party posts amid leadership discontent
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar has resigned from all party posts, including her district president role, citing moral responsibility for electoral defeats. Her move follows internal discussions about the party's setbacks and includes veiled criticism of political consultancy firm I-PAC's influence. A veteran leader, Dastidar's departure signals growing internal dissent within the TMC.

title: To avoid Rajasthan-like crisis, Congress opts for compromise over disruption in Karnataka
content: The Congress high command is reportedly seeking a compromise to avoid a Rajasthan-like political crisis in Karnataka by potentially replacing Chief Minister Siddaramaiah with Deputy CM DK Shivakumar. The party aims to manage competing power centers and caste equations carefully to prevent factional instability and protect its electoral prospects in 2028.

title: Ayushman Bharat, Education Policy Discussed At Delhi Workshop For Lawmakers
content: Healthcare reforms, implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and the growing role of digital communication in politics dominated discussions at a workshop attended by nearly 80 MPs and MLAs in the national capital on Monday.

title: In Vijay's 20-Minute Meeting With PM, Requests On State Anthem, Mekedatu
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay met with Prime Minister Narendra Modi today in Delhi on his first official visit to the national capital after coming to power earlier this month

title: Xi Vowed To Curb Smoking, But Cigarette Sales Tell A Different China Story
content: China's cigarette packaging also looks strikingly different from that in many countries. Instead of graphic health warnings, packs typically feature a single-line caution alongside national imagery such as pandas or the Gate of Heavenly Peace.

title: "Mamata Banerjee Overlooked Corruption": Longtime Aide Turns Critic
content: "Mamata Banerjee overlooked all corruption. And both Mamata and Abhishek Banerjee are responsible for such result," Partha Chatterjee told NDTV

title: Mumbai Boy Attacks Friend With Machete Over Alleged 'Chatting' With Mother
content: Since the accused is a minor, the police detained him for questioning and then sent him to the juvenile detention home in Dongri

title: Amid Eid Fervour In Kashmir, Jamia Mosque Closed Again For Prayers
content: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq, the chief cleric of the mosque, was also placed under house arrest ahead of Eid and strongly criticised the restrictions, calling them "a systematic assault on religious identity, dignity, and fundamental rights."

title: Ladakh Lieutenant Governor Warns Sonam Wangchuk Against "Misleading Narrative"
content: As political negotiations continue, the latest exchange between the Lieutenant Governor and Sonam Wangchuk underlines the growing tension between the Centre's push for development and local groups demanding stronger constitutional safeguards

title: Russian Lawmakers Want Banks, Their Staff To Help Fight Ukrainian Drones
content: Russian bank staff and branches could be joining the fight against Ukrainian drone attacks under an ambitious plan approved by the country's lower house of parliament.

title: Iran Says "Low" Possibility Of Return To War With US
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Wednesday that a return to war with the United States was unlikely, but warned the Islamic republic stood ready to respond to any attack.

title: Hormuz Oil Shock To Boost Electric Car Sales: World Energy Body
content: According to the IEA, the number of electric vehicles (excluding two-wheelers and three-wheelers) globally is projected to increase from approximately 80 million today to 510 million by 2035.

title: "Wife Not Maid, Marriage Not Service Contract": Court On Man's Divorce Plea
content: A bench comprising Justice Bharati Dangre and Justice Manjusha Deshpande held that a wife's refusal to perform daily household chores does not fall within the ambit of "mental cruelty".

title: Idli, Chutney And A Leadership Crisis: Karnataka's Breakfast Politics
content: The fact that a breakfast meeting between the two top leaders of the same party has to be publicly announced does evoke curiosity.

title: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Live Updates: Vijay Meets PM Modi In Delhi
content: Vijay Delhi Visit Live Updates: Vijay assumed office as Chief Minister on May 10 after his party emerged as the single largest party in the Assembly elections. TVK had won 108 seats in the 234-member Assembly

title: Opinion: Opinion | A 'Bangarappa' Redux? How Another Karnataka Chief Minister Was Asked To Resign In 1992
content: The images flashing on the TV screens on Tuesday reminded this author of a similar scenario in 1992, when S Bangarappa, Karnataka's first OBC Chief Minister, was asked to resign by PV Narasimha Rao.

title: West Bank On Edge As Israeli Army Steps Up Vigil In Samaria Amid Regional Tensions
content: NDTV travelled to Peduel in Samaria, where Israeli authorities say security forces are maintaining round the clock vigilance amid fears of infiltration attempts and attacks targeting Jewish communities in and around the West Bank.

title: Trinamool MP Quits Party Posts, Cites RG Kar In Bombshell Letter
content: Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar's letter also cited corruption in public distribution system, appointment of teachers, irregularities in governance and finance and the death of the death of the doctor in Kolkata's RG Kar hospital

title: Historic Move As 3 Private Players In Race To Build AMCA, India's Homegrown Stealth Fighter Jet
content: The Rs 15,000 crore project will see the winning private partner build five prototypes of the AMCA at a new greenfield facility in Andhra Pradesh

title: Video: Pinarayi Vijayan Supporters Attack Probe Team's Car, Smash Windshield
content: Personnel of a central police force were attacked with slippers by supporters of former Kerala chief minister Pinarayi Vijayan today as Enforcement Directorate officers came to search his house.

title: Opinion: Opinion | How Pak And Bangladeshi Vote Blocs Are Becoming UK's New 'Kingmakers'
content: In a February by-election in Gorton and Denton, a Manchester suburb, Pakistani voters played a major role in helping the Green Party come out on top, pushing Labour into third place behind Reform.

title: Pothundy twin murder case trial enters final phase
content: Case pertains to the murder of Sudhakaran and his mother Lakshmi at their house at Boyan Colony, Pothundy, near Nenmara, by their neighbour Chenthamara

title: Central University to offer six new postgraduate programmes
content: Vice-Chancellor Battu Satyanarayana announces a new ‘earn while learning’ initiative promoting self-reliance, skill development

title: SUCC urges Governor to halt DUK BoG meeting, questions Chairman’s legal tenure
content: Petition alleges May 29 Board meeting is “prima facie illegal” as BoG Chairman Prof. Vijay Chandru’s statutory term has expired

title: Heatwave eases briefly in Andhra Pradesh, severe conditions likely to return on May 28
content: Relief from temperatures above 45°C brings respite across most districts, but IMD and APSDMA warn of severe heatwave conditions in over 100 mandals on Thursday and Friday

title: Demography panel head says appointment came as a surprise to him
content: Retired Supreme Court judge Prakash Prabhakar Navlekar says he will call a meeting of all members soon, adds that demography and illegal migration are new subjects for him, and the scope of the study is vast

title: Amid leadership change developments, Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah receives caste survey report
content: This gains particular significance in light of Siddaramaiah’s legacy as a tall leader of the OBC bloc, and the report being viewed as part of his legacy

title: Five arrested after firing during land dispute in Dharwad
content: Police are investigating the case involving a clash and firing during the land dispute at Tadasinakoppa on the outskirts of Dharwad

title: Karnataka will go to mid-term polls, says BJP State president B.Y. Vijayendra
content: Mr. Vijayendra said the Congress high command’s marathon meeting in Delhi, which culminated in a directive to Siddaramaiah to step down, was an admission of the government’s failure.

title: NCW calls for legislation to codify Muslim personal law related to marriage
content: The report recommended prohibition of child marriage and regulation of divorce practices, ensuring fairness, due process, and equal access to remedies for women

title: Workshops held for poor performing govt. hospitals, explanations sought
content: Officials and medical staff from hospitals flagged through IVRS patient feedback were asked to explain lapses such as doctors’ absence, negligence in duties and sanitation shortcomings during review meetings in Amaravati

title: Rajasthan High Court confirms godman Asaram’s life sentence, drops gangrape charge
content: The court directed Asaram to surrender in view of the conviction; he is currently out on temporary bail, which was extended by seven days on May 25

title: Resonance Foundation Centre opened in Tirupati
content: The foundation centre will help students strengthen their basics and prepare for IIT-JEE, NEET, Olympiads and other competitive examinations with confidence, says managing director

title: Andhra Premier League season five to begin at YSR ACA-VDCA Stadium on June 9
content: Seven teams will play the fifth APL across Visakhapatnam, Kadapa and Mangalagiri in a round-robin format from June 9, with Simhadri Lions facing Kakinada Kings in the opener

title: Stalin, Kejriwal condemn raids, flay Congress for its questionable relations with BJP
content: CPI(M) General Secretary M.A. Baby’s questioned whether the UDF government was complicit in the raids at the residences of Pinarayi Vijayan

title: CPI seeks urgent relief as Anantapur farmers suffer pomegranate crop losses
content: In a letter addressed to the Chief Ministery, party’s State secretary says farmers who invested lakhs of rupees in horticulture crops by relying on loans were now left in distress due to massive crop losses

title: Tension in Thrissur following CPI(M) protest over ED raids
content: A section of protesters tears down flex boards put up by UDF; traffic on Swaraj Round hit. CPI(M) leaders allege ‘Congress-BJP deal,’ vow to intensify protest

title: Heatwave grips central, northwest India as monsoon stays offshore
content: Maximum temperatures ranged between 45°C and 47°C over Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, eastern Uttar Pradesh, Haryana and Vidarbha (Maharashtra), and between 40°C and 45°C across most of the rest of the country

title: BJP to organise awareness programmes on Modi’s 12-year governance from June 5 to 21
content: The Centre has introduced reforms aimed at economic growth and welfare, says Health Minister Satya Kumar Yadav and lashes out at Jagan for planning protests against coalition government in State

title: Cabinet approves technology-driven PDS scheme to strengthen food security
content: ‘SARTHAK-PDS does not replace the existing PDS system but will ensure structural reform across the delivery of foodgrains, logistics, transportation, material handling, and grievance redressal,’ Information and Broadcasting Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw says

title: Jagan asks YSRCP cadres to be vigilant of SIR exercise in Andhra Pradesh
content: Large-scale deletion of votes during SIR adversely affected the opposition parties in  Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, he says

title: Police report flags suspected gold thefts at Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple
content: The primary findings detail that nearly 78 grams of gold bars and coins, donated to the shrine as offerings by devotees, have gone missing recently

title: Supreme Court verdict on SIR, a defeat for Congress, allies: BJP
content: BJP MP Sudhanshu Trivedi says that after the SIR decision, Congress and Rahul Gandhi should introspect and stop using abusive language

title: JNTBGRI researchers discover rare new tree species in Kerala; named in honour of Prof. G.M. Nair
content: The newly discovered species were identified in the riparian forests of the Shendurney Wildlife Sanctuary

title: A.P. Rythu Sangham postpones State conference to July in view of heatwave
content: Farmers’ body announces village-level protests against MSPs declared by the Centre, seeks implementation of Swaminathan Commission recommendations and calls for remunerative prices for aqua and tobacco farmers

title: Being mute spectators no ground to prosecute in-laws for cruelty: SC
content: the in-laws of a woman cannot be prosecuted for cruelty or making dowry demands . such conduct is questionable on moral grounds, but does not automatically amount to criminal culpability . a bench of justices said that criminal law cannot be set in motion against every family member of the husband .

content: While not jumping with joy seeing every dog that comes her way (to the bewilderment of the dog owner and the dog), Haimanti fantasizes about fantasy books or classics to read and re-read.
